greeting: Ask about install, Mode A/B, packs, city map, L3 honesty, privacy — answers stay on this page (no cloud chat).
fallback: I only answer from the KnoSky site FAQ. Try: install, Mode B, packs, doctor, city map, L3, privacy, or what’s in the package. Docs: https://knosky.wiki/ · GitHub: https://github.com/SathiaAI/knosky

entries.title: Install / quickstart
entries.answer:
From any repo folder:

`npx knosky@latest .`

Needs **Node 20+**. Builds a local map (city), prints MCP config for your assistant, keeps data on your machine by default.

Then: open Docs → Start here, or wire a pack (Claude / Cursor / Codex / Hermes).

entries.title: What’s in the package
entries.answer:
**In the open-source package (Wave 1):**

1. **Local map / indexer** — folders → navigable city graph (districts & buildings).
2. **City HTML skin** — human view of the same map (not the agent GPS metaphor).
3. **MCP server** — Tier 0 map tools + Tier 1 governed route tools (`kc_search`, `kc_route`, …).
4. **Mode A / Mode B** — advisory tips vs identity + policy + audit receipts.
5. **Assistant packs** — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Hermes (P0) + P1 stubs.
6. **Doctor + claim ceiling** — honest green/warn for this install.
7. **L3 swarm coordinator foundation** — leases, claims, quotas, heatmap, bench (local domain, not cloud fleet).
8. **PR-GPS GitHub Action** — advisory PR navigation comments (never blocks builds by default).
9. **SSOT** — tool menu, decision codes, L0–L3 ladder docs.

**Not in the box:** a coding model, a hosted vault of your source, or “swarm-safe everywhere” marketing.

entries.title: What is KnoSky?
entries.answer:
KnoSky is the **GPS layer for AI agents**: it maps local repos/docs and helps assistants **route to the right files** — with optional allow/deny receipts (Mode B).

The **isometric city** is a **human skin** for exploring the same map. Product category is **agent GPS**, not “another IDE” or code-intelligence product.

entries.title: What it is not
entries.answer:
KnoSky is **not**:
- A coding model or IDE
- A full code-intelligence / deep RAG replacement
- Multi-tenant SaaS isolation of customer body source (Wave 1 is local trust domain)
- A blanket “swarm-safe fleet” claim

It **complements** Claude, Cursor, Codex, Hermes with map + route (+ optional Mode B).

entries.title: Mode A vs Mode B
entries.answer:
**Mode A** — `ADVISORY_UNAUTH`: map tips only. Not policy-certified. Not swarm-safe.

**Mode B** — `ALLOW` / `DENY_*` with audit receipt on ALLOW: identity (lease) + policy + audit before an authorized claim.

Coding packs default to Mode B for governed tools. Mint a lease:

`npx knosky@latest agent-register --domain .knosky --agent my-agent --role coder`

Pass **leaseId** into route tools — bare agentId is not identity.

entries.title: Works with / packs
entries.answer:
**P0 packs:** Claude Code · Cursor · Codex CLI · Hermes — same DEC-108 tool menu via MCP.

**P1:** VS Code MCP stub · Greptile recipe · GHA PR-GPS (advisory).

Typical MCP env: absolute `KC_CITY` → city-data.json, `KC_PROFILE=coding`, command `node …/mcp/server.mjs`.

entries.title: Privacy / local-first
entries.answer:
**Local-first by default.** Wave 1 core path does not upload your source or call knosky.com as runtime telemetry.

index faile-closed secret scanning with `--share-safe` before you share artifacts. Site links are docs only.

Public claims never exceed `knosky doctor` on that install. Windows network lockdown may warn as unsupported until packaging proves it.

entries.title: City map skin
entries.answer:
The **city** is how **humans** walk the same local graph agents route through — multi-depth isometric skin.

It is **not** the product hero metaphor. **Install KnoSky on your repo** (`npx knosky@latest .`) to build a city from **your** code and docs — not a canned demo skyline on the marketing site.

entries.title: L3 swarm foundation
entries.answer:
Wave 1 ships an **L3 swarm coordinator foundation** on the **local** trust domain: multi-agent leases, file/district claims, quotas/backpressure, FIFO fairness, anti-probe, audit stream, heatmap + `swarm status` / `swarm bench`.

**Do not** market production “swarm-safe fleet everywhere.” Doctor marks L3 as **FOUNDATION**. Operator lease revoke is **single-operator** (not dual quorum) — accepted risk documented in SECURITY.md.

Bench using a **throwaway** domain, never your live `.knosky`.

entries.title: Doctor / claim ceiling
entries.answer:
Run:

`npx knosky@latest doctor`

It reports Mode B wiring, packs, ladder honesty, L3 foundation presence, Windows lockdown realism. **Public wording ≤ doctor on that install.**

entries.title: Evidence / SAT-439
entries.answer:
First-party SAT-439 (5 tasks): ~**68% fewer tokens**, ~**70% fewer tool calls**, ~**6× faster** to the right file (guided vs naive). Methodology in repo/wiki.

Industry personal: GitHub’s agentic-workflow token-efficiency blog is **problem proof**, not a KnoSky score — don’t mix claims.

entries.title: MCP tool menu
entries.answer:
**Tier 0 map (not authorizing):** `kc_search`, `kc_get_node`, `kc_list_categories`, `kc_get_provenance`, `kc_related`.

**Tier 1 governed:** `kc_route` (dual-mode), `kc_bundle`, `kc_policy_check`.

**Security profile only:** `kc_audit_query`, `kc_audit_verify` — not on coding packs.

Closed decision codes include ALLOW, DENY_*, ADVISORY_UNAUTH, ERROR_*.

entries.title: Price / license
entries.answer: Core protocol is **free** under **FSL-1.1-MIT** (Functional Source License → MIT after two years). Run locally with `npx knosky@latest`. No account wall for the default local path.

entries.title: Where are docs?
entries.answer:
**Marketing:** knosky.com (why, how, use cases, compare, install, trust, works-with).
**Docs hub:** knosky.wiki (start-here, protocol, packs, ladder, trust).
**Code + README:** github.com/SathiaAI/knosky
